question: [File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getScheduleById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the wait_for_expiry=true field in a schedule response to determine that a freeze schedule will execute at expiration_time regardless of collected signatures, and use this information to calculate the exact timestamp when funds will be permanently frozen, enabling a precisely timed front-running attack?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the cache key does not include the Authorization header (cacheKeyGenerator uses only req.originalUrl) to cause a response cached from an authenticated request (with a higher limit showing more freeze data) to be served to unauthenticated users, or vice versa, creating an information asymmetry about frozen token states?
